title: Just watched 1 2 my opinion
wikitext:
[]: Just watched 1-2, my opinion. Post by Naminator » Fri Nov 12, 2010 5:48 pm Well hello Eva monkeys, or w/e you guys prefer to call yourselves. I think I'll give you a little disclaimer first. Yes I am an Evangelion fan, I might not be one of you or can be called a "true" fan, but fan none the less. This is my first time posting here so take it easy on me if I am doing something wrong. Most of my time on this site is spent looking at the caption threads and such, for some Evangelion themed lolz.(no one is ever going to let the whole Shinji masturbation thing go, are they?) Anyways I just finished watching the Evangelion Rebuild 1.11 and 2.22(subs), and it really blew me away to be honest, I don't think I have ever seen an anime this good before. Let me start off with things I like. First thing I just absolutely LOVE is of course the ultimate depussification of Shinji. I'm sure I'm not the only one who just absolutely hated Shinji's guts in the original anime series. He was whiny and annoying, constantly bitching about people not wanting him or some other BS even though he received a lot of praise and love from the people around him, with the obvious exception, Asuka. But I guess she is doing it out of love, so she can be forgiven. Furthermore Shinji was F'ing STUPID in the original series, I'm sorry but I have yet to see such a clueless character in any anime that isn't purely comedic. No wonder Asuka kept calling him baka! In the anime movies he seemed more assertive, more in control of himself and while a little on the soft side, it's no where near how bad it was in the original series. So yeaaaa, I believe that was an excellent direction to move the series to. Second thing I liked a lot. The graphics! Yea, yea, say what you like, but I'm a graphic whore and I have never seen an anime create such great presentation. And it's not all about how colorful and shiny the things are, I'm talking the animations as well. Everything was so fluid and beautiful, the usually copy/paste animation BS that is in every anime was gone and so were the extreme facial/body exaggerations. You combine all of that with great attention to detail and you are on your way to creating a great anime that people can watch and enjoy even if they don't get the whole story, just like the movies do it. Third thing I really liked was how all the characters changed. I really like the way things are going now, the characters in general are much more likable. I honestly was getting tired how the original series made everyone to be as though they escaped a mental institution recently. It's ok to have deep characters, but you can't go overboard on it, the characters need be relatable and likable or else you wont care for them. There were a few times when I could have related to Shinji or Asuka, but then in an episode or two they would just go fucking nuts for the stupidest reasons and what ever slight connection I had with the characters would quickly disappear as all the growth they aquired during their journey and time together disappear in a blink of an eye, and they would sink even more in their insanity. Now Asuka and Rei are much more likable/bearable, they act as they should, kids who are much more mature for their age but at the same time suffer from childish mentalities. Like Asuka getting jealous of Rei and trying to cook for Shinji, that was pretty cute and it showed a better side of her, rather than the usual "Ill kick everyone's ass because Im the best/I'm going to be all depressed and semi-vegetable because I have issues" BS. Rei also seems to be more social and shows signs of humanity this time around, and she does not get replaced by a replacement clone, which makes the character much more likable, because lets face it, who the hell cared for Rei after they found out that the old one is dead and the new one is just a clone with barely any memories of the old Rei. Fourth would be the Angels and the battles. Well I must say that I approve of the new aproach, lol how could I not? The battles are intense, they FINALLY added some damn teamwork in to the fight. The old Evangelion had only one team fight and any fights other than the ones that involved Shinji were usually over within seconds. It's like other Evas only existed as filler/time sink. Asuka killed one Angel by herself, then they had excellent team fight against that suicide-bomber Angel. Then Rei and Maya(Mari?) started an excellent fight against that last Angel in 2.22 which was just excellent and, again, not the usual "Hi durrr Angel, Bye durrr Angel, say hi to Shinji for me when he is ripping out your skull". Overall the new Angels and their battles were just simply amazing and I loved them. The final thing of course would be the lack of filler. While the concept of having filler in a 26 episode anime is a bit absurd, the original series did have it's fair share of it. And I like how the movie does not waste our time with it. Knowing that Evangelion is a major attraction in Japan Im sure that the producer could have jerked us around with filler just so he can release an extra part and cash in. Im glad that that is not the case here, everything is as straight forward as it can be, we are either gazing upon a beautiful and heart pounding action scene, revisiting some of the best moments from the old series or learning a thing or two about the characters and their world, which is a great thing. Looking at all of this, there are a few things that i didn't like, but the list is pretty short. One would be how they changed Asuka's name. Yea sorry not buying THAT, her name is Asuka Langley Soryu, and she will forever remain that way to me. The other most important issue for me is that THERE IS ONLY 2 MOVIES OUT! DAMN IT! I WANT MORE! xD Well anyway those are my thoughts. And I want to leave you guys with a question. Does anyone know when the 3.0 will be out? Or at the very least tell me if it is being worked on? I heard somewhere that they are not even working on it that I think that would just crush my soul. Also, as I was buying the 1.11 BlueRay from amazon, one of the people who reviewed 1.11 said this "it is scheduled to be presented in 4 parts total, with the third and fourth parts being released together". Is there any truth to this? And if yes, then I must say that this fills me with fear and excitement. Fear of the taking forever and a half to release the damn thing and excitement that I'll get to enjoy 2 more Eva movies. Thanks in advance and if you have any problem understanding me then feel free to ask, English is not my first language so I might have miscommunicated something. Take care and have a nice day fellow eva fans.

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Nov 12, 2010 7:52 pm I think it's simply best to not think of things here in terms of 'improved' and 'worse' or 'weak' and 'badass'. I'm pretty sure that true appreciation of these movies will be found in viewing the differences in actions and circumstances and examining what comes from that. To do otherwise would be to miss the point, I think.

I disagree completely and I'm sure plenty other people will, too. I can't speak for anyone else, but the reason I found the characters in NGE to be so likeable and relatable was because they were so fucked up. Y'know, just like real people. I'm not going to go into too much of this here because we have the entire Discussion and Chit Chat forums devoted to it.

[]: Post by Azathoth » Fri Nov 12, 2010 9:15 pm ETA: you know what, I don't fucking care. Not gonna feed the troll. OP, I disagree with many of your opinions, but whatever, the longer I stay in this thread the more likely I am to say off-topic shit.

[]: Post by Defectron » Fri Nov 12, 2010 9:25 pm Noooooooo!

[]: Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Nov 12, 2010 9:34 pm oh yeah, I forgot about the high octane nightmare fuel stuff left on the cutting room floor that was some great stuff and could've added a much needed swift kick in the nuts to the series and assisted on the tone transition first half is (mostly) upbeat, but once Asuka gets into Unit 03, the film pretty much blasts off orbit and out of left field, signifying that the shit has just hit the fan, for better or worse let's hope the remaining two films actually take note of these concepts and takes advantage of the business 2.0 did at the Japanese Box Office (i.e. you're on the pedestal of financial fame and can get away with much more than before without opposition) I will say that I do hope that Rebuild does end on a upbeat note. I'm just saying that it would be very rewarding if the cast goes through the darkest depths of hell and for making it through the other side, is rewarding with the positive aspects of life and whatnot

[]: What's that? Is it a flying dick? No, it's Killer Bee! Post by Killer Bee » Fri Nov 12, 2010 10:35 pm Naminator wrote: Anyway, don't you think thats a bit of an exaggeration there?

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Nov 12, 2010 11:02 pm First of all hi Naminator and welcome; it's nice to see someone else who liked the Rebuild About Shinji (without I quote everything): he was an interesting character back in the 90s but he was already an half-copy of Nadia, more whiny characters were just going to set us in yawn mode for around 2 hours per movie; I can only imagine what could have been written here if Shinji (or other aspects of the movie) was identical to the one present in NGE: "yeah, nice animation and music but it's just a remake, you know nothing that will say something of new, you can safely skip it" Or maybe some guys are so drugged with NGE that could have ended up liking a simply updated remake of NGE? Is it really possible? About the graphics: you're absolutely right; I was initially negative about the Rebuild (because I thought that it was going to be "merely a remake") but before I could even enjoy the story I just told myself: "WTF! This is the most visually impressive anime I've ever seen! At least I must give it a shot!". The visual aspect is always important in animation, of course I've liked slow animes with shitty and/or outdated animations (actually I liked a lot of them) but an anime is much more enjoyable when it features nice animations and a good pace. About the changed characters: I've liked as well the new characters but of course an Asuka fan will probably be disappointed by the few space that she get (actually you seem a fan of Asuka yourself, it wasn't a problem for you?). This has been a discussed issue of the Rebuild, the movies end up focusing mainly (almost only) on Shinji and other characters were reduced in importance. But actually wasn't Evangelion Shinji's story? The endings (both of them, 26 and 26') focus only on Shinji. Changing the approach since the beginning could simply be a result of Anno's own decision that it was either impossible or futile to focus on more than one character since he's telling the story from the point of view of the protagonist. Of course the characters are updated to today standards and they're more "moe" but I find them nicer and more enjoyable (probably because they're closer to the idea that many viewers ended up having of those characters; or at least it's my opinion (since the beginning Eva's characters had more possible interpretation and the fans ended up taking the one that they preferred, just look at the many doujins/fanfictions and you can notice it; probably this shifting caused the loss of that balance but it's not impossible that in the end there could be a new balance; right now it's just "that's not (MY) Rei; that's not (MY) Asuka" and so on, people are influenced too much by the old version and can't even try to enjoy the new one). About the battles: they're nicer but actually I didn't see that much of additional teamwork; Rei and Mari helping each other is just one more way to show as much Rei changed but other than that it's the same so far, Shinji defeated two Angels by himself, against Ramiel there was the Yashima operation with the 00 backing up the 01, Mari and Asuka each got a solo battle and then Sahaquiel was defeated in team. Of course the way the battles are depicted has been updated and this change of course changes the "feeling" that you've while you're watching them but this is more or less the same that can be said with the animation. I think that the format that they're using is interesting, the movies give me an intense experience from the beginning to the ending, it can't be the same for a TV series (while of course it has disadvantages as well, the reduction of focus on secondary characters could be partially caused by the format). Naminator wrote: Looking at all of this, there are a few things that i didn't like, but the list is pretty short. One would be how they changed Asuka's name. Yea sorry not buying THAT, her name is Asuka Langley Soryu, and she will forever remain that way to me.

[]: Post by Nonoriri » Sat Nov 13, 2010 1:31 pm MasonFighter wrote: I'm able to thoroughly enjoy the Rebuild movies without giving it any thought because I accept that they're worse than the series.

[]: Post by Sharaz Destler » Sat Nov 13, 2010 3:02 pm Eh, for some reason I never liked "Soryu" as Asuka's surname. It just sounds a bit weird, but "Shikinami" is easier (for me, at least) to pronounce without garbling it.

But I guess she is doing it out of love, so she can be forgiven. Furthermore Shinji was F'ing STUPID in the original series, I'm sorry but I have yet to see such a clueless character in any anime that isn't purely comedic. No wonder Asuka kept calling him baka!

But I guess she is doing it out of love, so she can be forgiven. Furthermore Shinji was F'ing STUPID in the original series, I'm sorry but I have yet to see such a clueless character in any anime that isn't purely comedic. No wonder Asuka kept calling him baka! So yeaaaa, I believe that was an excellent direction to move the series to. Second thing I liked a lot. The graphics! Yea, yea, say what you like, but I'm a graphic whore and I have never seen an anime create such great presentation. Third thing I really liked was how all the characters changed. I really like the way things are going now, the characters in general are much more likable. I honestly was getting tired how the original series made everyone to be as though they escaped a mental institution recently. It's ok to have deep characters, but you can't go overboard on it, the characters need be relatable and likable or else you wont care for them. There were a few times when I could have related to Shinji or Asuka, but then in an episode or two they would just go fucking nuts for the stupidest reasons and what ever slight connection I had with the characters would quickly disappear as all the growth they aquired during their journey and time together disappear in a blink of an eye, and they would sink even more in their insanity. It's like other Evas only existed as filler/time sink. Asuka killed one Angel by herself, then they had excellent team fight against that suicide-bomber Angel. Then Rei and Maya(Mari?) started an excellent fight against that last Angel in 2.22 which was just excellent and, again, not the usual "Hi durrr Angel, Bye durrr Angel, say hi to Shinji for me when he is ripping out your skull". Im glad that that is not the case here, everything is as straight forward as it can be, we are either gazing upon a beautiful and heart pounding action scene, revisiting some of the best moments from the old series or learning a thing or two about the characters and their world, which is a great thing. Looking at all of this, there are a few things that i didn't like, but the list is pretty short. One would be how they changed Asuka's name. Yea sorry not buying THAT, her name is Asuka Langley Soryu, and she will forever remain that way to me. The other most important issue for me is that THERE IS ONLY 2 MOVIES OUT! DAMN IT! Is there any truth to this? And if yes, then I must say that this fills me with fear and excitement. Take care and have a nice day fellow eva fans.

Is it really possible? About the graphics: you're absolutely right; I was initially negative about the Rebuild (because I thought that it was going to be "merely a remake") but before I could even enjoy the story I just told myself: "WTF! This is the most visually impressive anime I've ever seen! At least I must give it a shot!". The visual aspect is always important in animation, of course I've liked slow animes with shitty and/or outdated animations (actually I liked a lot of them) but an anime is much more enjoyable when it features nice animations and a good pace. About the changed characters: I've liked as well the new characters but of course an Asuka fan will probably be disappointed by the few space that she get (actually you seem a fan of Asuka yourself, it wasn't a problem for you?). This has been a discussed issue of the Rebuild, the movies end up focusing mainly (almost only) on Shinji and other characters were reduced in importance. But actually wasn't Evangelion Shinji's story? The endings (

Article: Just watched 1 2 my opinion
 problem for you?). This has been a discussed issue of the Rebuild, the movies end up focusing mainly (almost only) on Shinji and other characters were reduced in importance. But actually wasn't Evangelion Shinji's story? The endings (both of them, 26 and 26') focus only on Shinji. Changing the approach since the beginning could simply be a result of Anno's own decision that it was either impossible or futile to focus on more than one character since he's telling the story from the point of view of the protagonist. Of course the characters are updated to today standards and they're more "moe" but I find them nicer and more enjoyable (probably because they're closer to the idea that many viewers ended up having of those characters; or at least it's my opinion (since the beginning Eva's characters had more possible interpretation and the fans ended up taking the one that they preferred, just look at the many doujins/fanfictions and you can notice it; probably this shifting caused the loss of that balance but it's not impossible that in the end there could be a new balance; right now it's just "that's not (MY) Rei; that's not (MY) Asuka" and so on, people are influenced too much by the old version and can't even try to enjoy the new one). About the battles: they're nicer but actually I didn't see that much of additional teamwork; Rei and Mari helping each other is just one more way to show as much Rei changed but other than that it's the same so far, Shinji defeated two Angels by himself, against Ramiel there was the Yashima operation with the 00 backing up the 01, Mari and Asuka each got a solo battle and then Sahaquiel was defeated in team. Of course the way the battles are depicted has been updated and this change of course changes the "feeling" that you've while you're watching them but this is more or less the same that can be said with the animation. I think that the format that they're using is interesting, the movies give me an intense experience from the beginning to the ending, it can't be the same for a TV series (while of course it has disadvantages as well, the reduction of focus on secondary characters could be partially caused by the format).  well, the reduction of focus on secondary characters could be partially caused by the format).
